stub: He is a Labour MP who comes from a white working class background.
personalization: He frequently speaks out and votes against his party leadership.
substantive: He opposes increasing the basic Universal Credit grant by £20 per week.
justification: In political debates, he regularly explains how his decisions help to promote the interests of people like you (e.g. of your class and educational background).
responsiveness: Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, he said: “I did not go into politics to win elections, but to make this country a better place.”
text: He is a Labour MP who comes from a white working class background. He is from your constituency. He frequently speaks out and votes against his party leadership. He opposes increasing the basic Universal Credit grant by £20 per week. In political debates, he regularly explains how his decisions help to promote the interests of people like you (e.g. of your class and educational background). Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, he said: “I did not go into politics to win elections, but to make this country a better place.”
